text:
There once was a young girl named Sophie. She loved anything fluffy and soft. One day, she heard wonderful tales of Owsla, the owl league of fluffy owl warriors.

Driven by ambition, Sophie joined. They trained all night, dreaming big.

The first big hunt? Hundreds of feathers just as soft as hers.

The feathers were dark as midnight. Sophie's eyes grew wide.

The winners?

Three big owls.

They wore silken feathers.

Sophie felt proud.

Every night she dreamed.

The Owls?

Sheaven.

Their feathers shimmered like stars.

Then came winter.

A terrible great snow owl.

Feathers icy blue.

Her howl was like a storm.

She'd never win.

But at dawn—

A snowflake from her wing fell into her bowl.

It wasn't white. It was soft grey.

They liked each other.

Winter turned to spring.

Those grey feathers?

Tapestrips.

They grew. They shimmered.

Now those owls? They
